title: Store alias path traversal escapes by-alias symlink directory
description: Mounted store aliases are validated only as non-empty strings, but syncStoreAliasLinks and detectAliasLinkDrift use alias directly in join(byAliasDir, alias). An alias containing '../' or path separators can make doctor/store operations create, remove, or inspect symlinks outside ~/.fabric/stores/by-alias, causing unsafe filesystem mutation in the user's Fabric home.
fix_direction: Constrain MountedStore.alias and storeIdentity.canonical_alias to a safe single path segment such as /^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$/ and reject '.', '..', slash and backslash; also defensively resolve link paths and verify they remain inside byAliasDir before rmSync/symlinkSync/lstatSync.

context.notes: Discovered by multi-perspective discovery DBP-20260608-215329

title: Store-era recall recomputes and rereads the full read-set corpus multiple times per request
description: The hot recall path computes the store corpus revision, then separately builds raw candidates, and get_sections repeats the revision plus body-index build. All four calls funnel through walkReadSetStores(), which walks readKnowledgeAcrossStores() and readFileSyncs every store markdown file, so a normal fab_recall -> fab_get_knowledge_sections round trip can perform four full read-set walks/full-file reads before reading selected bodies again. Cost scales with total mounted store corpus size on every request despite the code already having the in-memory selection token and corpus revision opportunities for reuse.
fix_direction: Build the read-set corpus once per request (or cache it by store directory fingerprint/max mtime) and thread the entries/revision/body index through plan-context and knowledge-sections; at minimum derive computeReadSetRevision, raw items, and body index from a shared walk result so each store file is read once.

context.notes: Discovered by multi-perspective discovery DBP-20260608-215329

issue_history.note: Added fingerprinted read-set walk cache and regression coverage for revision/raw/body-index reuse.

resolution: Resolved by adding a read-set corpus cache keyed by project/FABRIC_HOME plus per-file size and mtime fingerprints. computeReadSetRevision, buildCrossStoreRawItems, and buildCrossStoreBodyIndex now reuse the same walked markdown corpus across a request chain while file changes invalidate the cache before revision recomputation.

title: Corrupt store counters fail open to zero and can re-mint existing stable_ids
description: readStoreCounters returns default zero counters when counters.json is unreadable, invalid JSON, or schema-invalid, and allocateStoreKnowledgeId immediately uses that value inside the lock before writing nextCounters. If counters.json is corrupted while knowledge files with higher IDs still exist, the next approval can mint KT/KP IDs that already exist, corrupting cite identity and file naming invariants.
fix_direction: Make allocation fail closed on corrupt counters.json, preserve a .corrupted sidecar, and require/perform reconcileStoreCounters before minting. Only return zero counters for an absent file, not for parse/schema corruption on the write path.

context.notes: Discovered by multi-perspective discovery DBP-20260608-215329

resolution: Allocation now fails closed on corrupt or schema-invalid counters.json, preserves a forensic sidecar, and requires reconcile before minting another stable_id.

title: store migrate removes sources before counters are reconciled, leaving an interruption window for duplicate IDs
description: store-migrate writes imported entries into target stores, then removes the project sources, and only afterwards calls reconcileStoreCounters. Any crash, process kill, disk error, or interrupted command between the target writes/source removal and counter reconciliation leaves the store containing imported stable_ids while counters.json remains stale, so the next allocateStoreKnowledgeId can re-mint those IDs.
fix_direction: Make migration transactional: reconcile/atomically write counters for each target before committing/removing sources, or write a migration receipt and recover/reconcile on the next run before any allocation. Use atomic writes and fail closed if counter reconciliation cannot complete.

context.notes: Discovered by multi-perspective discovery DBP-20260608-215329

issue_history.note: Resolved by retiring the entire fabric store migrate surface in commit 30b5cbe: the public subcommand, store-migrate implementation, and legacy migration tests were deleted, so the interrupted source-removal/counter-reconciliation window no longer exists.

resolution: Resolved by retiring the entire fabric store migrate surface in commit 30b5cbe: the public subcommand, store-migrate implementation, and legacy migration tests were deleted, so the interrupted source-removal/counter-reconciliation window no longer exists.

title: Global/project store config mutations use unlocked read-modify-write and can lose concurrent updates
description: storeAdd/storeCreate/storeRemove and storeBind/storeSwitchWrite load config, mutate in memory, and save with plain writeFileSync through saveGlobalConfig/saveProjectConfig. Two concurrent CLI operations against the same global or project config can both read the same base state and the later save overwrites the earlier mount/bind/write-target update; interruption during write can also leave a truncated config that later loadGlobalConfig/loadProjectConfig parses without recovery.
fix_direction: Wrap global/project config read-modify-write operations in withFileLock keyed by the config path and persist via atomicWriteJson with a corruption sidecar/recovery path. Consider helper APIs that combine load-mutate-save under one lock.

title: Retired install implementation remains beside live install-v2 and is still tested
description: The command registry routes production `fabric install` to `./install-v2.js`, but the older `packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts` still exports a full `installCommand` and `runInitCommand` implementation with the same command metadata. Tests still import the retired module directly, so behavior can diverge between tested legacy code and the shipped command path, making maintenance and regression triage ambiguous.
fix_direction: Either delete/quarantine `commands/install.ts` and migrate the remaining tests/helpers to `install-v2` or shared install pipeline modules, or make `install.ts` a thin compatibility re-export of the live implementation so there is only one install behavior to maintain.

title: Client hook scripts are checked in as four identical source copies
description: The package contains the template hook plus identical `.claude`, `.codex`, and `.cursor` hook copies; for example `fabric-hint.cjs` is present under `templates/hooks/` and all three client directories with identical hashes. title: install-v2 runs semantic-search guidance twice after successful pipeline install
description: The new install pipeline includes GuidanceStage, whose documented responsibility includes handling semantic search and whose execute path prompts/enables it. After pipeline success, runInitCommand preserves the old post-install semantic-search block and calls the same prompt/enabling logic again. In an interactive install this can ask the same question twice; with --enable-embed it can print duplicate enablement guidance in one command run.
fix_direction: Move semantic-search onboarding to a single owner. Prefer keeping it inside GuidanceStage and deleting the legacy post-install block in install-v2, or make GuidanceStage purely presentational and keep all mutation prompts after the pipeline.

title: `fabric info scope` without scope argument falls through to runtime crash
description: The `scope` positional is optional, but the scope branch calls `runScopeExplain(args.scope!)`. The scope validator uses RegExp.test(scope), and the shared regex accepts the coerced string `undefined`; the built dist then reaches resolver code and throws a raw TypeError stack trace instead of showing usage or an actionable missing-argument error.
fix_direction: Require the scope positional when subcommand is `scope`, or explicitly guard in resolveMode/runScopeExplain and render `fabric info scope <scope>` usage with a nonzero exit before calling scopeExplain.

context.notes: Discovered by multi-perspective discovery DBP-20260608-215329

issue_history.note: Resolved by guarding fabric info scope before resolver dispatch: missing scope prints Usage: fabric info scope <scope>, sets exitCode=1, and returns without throwing a raw runtime error. Verified by info command test.

resolution: Resolved by guarding fabric info scope before resolver dispatch: missing scope prints Usage: fabric info scope <scope>, sets exitCode=1, and returns without throwing a raw runtime error. Verified by info command test.

title: Ink TUI status states rely on color and Unicode glyphs instead of spoken text labels
description: Status rendering hard-codes color maps and symbol maps, then renders only the glyph as the status cue; running/success/error/skipped state is not emitted as text for screen readers or color-blind users. This affects install pipeline step output and TUI status messages, separate from the shared colors.ts fallback path.
fix_direction: Render textual state labels such as [running], [ok], [warn], [error], [skipped] alongside glyphs in all TUI modes, or route TUI status indicators through the shared accessible symbol/label helpers.

title: Install TUI spinner animates Braille frames continuously with no reduced-motion or screen-reader-safe mode

fix_direction: Add a reduced-motion/text-only mode that renders a stable 'Running: <stage>' line, and have the install pipeline or renderer choose it when color/animation is disabled or when an explicit config/env option requests accessible output.

title: OutputRendererConfig.colors is declared but InkOutputRenderer never applies it
description: The public renderer config exposes colors?: boolean, but InkOutputRenderer only stores the config and renderComponent calls Ink render(node) without passing or propagating color preferences; child components hard-code Text color props. A caller cannot force text-only/no-color TUI output through this config.
fix_direction: Plumb the color flag into TUI components or renderer context, suppress Ink color props when false, and add tests proving createInkRenderer({ colors: false }) produces text-only output.

title: Session digests persist raw user transcript snippets without redaction or retention controls
description: Stop-hook transcript summarization pushes raw user message text into user_messages, then session-digest-writer writes up to 10 messages of 500 chars each under .fabric/.cache/session-digests. The writer only truncates; it does not call the existing secret/PII redaction utilities and no TTL cleanup is configured for these digest files, so sensitive prompts can linger in local cache and later feed fabric-archive context.
fix_direction: Run digest content through the shared redactSecrets/privacy filter before write, add a configurable TTL/cleanup for .fabric/.cache/session-digests, and prefer summaries over verbatim user-message bullets.

title: fab_review lifecycle events lack invoker session provenance
description: FabReviewInputSchema branches for approve/reject/defer contain only pending paths/reason/until and no session_id or actor field; the service then emits knowledge_proposed, knowledge_promote_started, knowledge_promoted, knowledge_rejected, and knowledge_deferred events with timestamp/reason but no session_id/correlation_id. The ledger records that a knowledge write happened but cannot attribute it to the invoking session or operator.
fix_direction: Add optional session_id/correlation_id (and, if available, actor/client) to fab_review input, thread it through reviewKnowledge, and include it on all review mutation events; add tests asserting approve/reject/defer/promote events preserve provenance.

title: Archive secret scan omits persisted path metadata fields
description: extractKnowledge gates secret-shaped content by scanning user_messages_summary, session_context, must_read_if, and intent_clues only. However recent_paths are written verbatim into the pending entry Evidence block and evidence_paths are written into frontmatter, so credential-like or PII-bearing path strings can still land in a git-backed knowledge store despite the comment that secrets must never be persisted.
fix_direction: Include recent_paths, evidence_paths, relevance_paths, tags, tech_stack, and impact in the secret/privacy scan before rendering; redact or reject sensitive path metadata and add regression tests covering token-looking paths.

title: apply-tag-version rewrites quarantined private package versions unlike sync-versions

Evidence: scripts/sync-versions.mjs:62, scripts/sync-versions.mjs:64, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:40, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:50, scripts/apply-tag-version.mjs:53, packages/server-http-experimental/package.json:3, packages/server-http-experimental/package.json:4.
fix_direction: Make apply-tag-version share the same manifest collection/filter as sync-versions, skipping private packages or at least preserving 0.0.0-quarantine for the quarantined package.

title: fabric store switch-write lacks end-to-end coverage for resolved-bindings snapshot refresh
description: The public onboarding flow tells users to run `fabric store bind` and then `fabric store switch-write`, and hooks read the resolved-bindings snapshot for read/write labels. `bind` refreshes that snapshot, but the `switch-write` command only calls `storeSwitchWrite` and prints success; the helper test asserts only active/default write config fields. fix_direction: Add a command-level integration test that seeds two mounted writable stores, runs the public `store switch-write` command, reads the resolved-bindings snapshot, and asserts `write_target.alias` changes. Update the command to call `regenerateBindingsSnapshot(projectRoot, ...)` after `storeSwitchWrite`; consider the same assertion for `route-write` when write routes affect snapshot resolution.

title: Store-aware MCP contract advertises provenance and written_to_store fields that runtime outputs do not produce
description: `MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS` and its tests lock a 6-tool store-aware contract where READ tools surface `global_ref`/`provenance` and WRITE tools echo `written_to_store`, and the parity matrix describes recall/sections as returning `store_uuid + alias + local_id + global_ref`. title: Project route-write is ignored by archive/review write-target resolution
description: Invariant: after `fabric store route-write project:fabric-v2 platform`, a team-layer archive in a repo bound to `active_project=fabric-v2` should land in the routed `platform` store and record matching visibility. The shared resolver supports project routes (`packages/shared/test/store/privacy-boundary.test.ts:55`), and the CLI persists routes (`packages/cli/src/store/store-ops.ts:476`), but server write-target helpers always resolve the literal `team` scope for team-layer writes (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:60`, `packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:115`) before later stamping `semantic_scope: project:<active_project>` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:123`). `extractKnowledge` and review promotion/layer-flip use those helpers (`packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:359`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:607`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:964`), so the config write round-trip silently falls back to the default/active team store instead of the route.
fix_direction: Derive an effective write scope once (`personal` for personal, otherwise `project:${active_project}` when present, else `team`) and pass that scope into `resolveWriteTarget` for both target dir and visibility metadata. Add an integration test that seeds two writable stores, sets `write_routes: [{scope:'project:fabric-v2', store:'platform'}]` plus `active_project:'fabric-v2'`, runs `extractKnowledge` and review approve, then asserts pending/canonical paths, counters, and `visibility_store` land in `platform`.

title: fab_recall include_related only works with store-qualified related IDs despite bare-id contract
description: Invariant: a frontmatter edge authored as `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` should round-trip through plan candidates and `fab_recall({include_related:true})` to fetch that neighbor. The schema/builder document and parse bare stable ids (`packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts:80`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1070`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1245`), while cross-store recall emits store-qualified candidate ids via `entry.qualifiedId` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-recall.ts:163`). `recall` compares `candidate.description.related` directly against store-qualified `candidateIds` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:132`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:137`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:148`), and the test only passes because it seeds a non-contract `related: [team:KT-GLD-0001]` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.test.ts:196`). Real bare related edges therefore neither expand nor produce the next-step nudge.
fix_direction: Normalize related ids before comparison: accept bare local ids by qualifying them with the source candidate's store alias when possible, or compare against both local and qualified id maps. Update recall tests to seed `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` and add cross-store ambiguity coverage so include_related behavior is deterministic.

title: In-flight drain uses a single waiter slot, so concurrent shutdown drains can overwrite each other
description: createShutdownHandler is per-signal, so SIGTERM and SIGINT handlers can both enter the first-invocation path and call tracker.drain concurrently. The tracker stores only one resolveDrained callback; the later drain overwrites the earlier one, so when active requests exit only the newest waiter resolves immediately while the earlier shutdown path waits until its deadline and may then run fsync/flush/close/exit out of phase with the other handler.
fix_direction: Represent drain waiters as a Set/array and resolve all pending waiters when active.size reaches 0, or centralize shutdown dedup across all signals so only one drain can be active; add a regression test for concurrent drain() calls before the final exit().

title: Windows store paths let pending modify-layer bypass the approve gate
description: modifyLayerFlip intends to reject layer flips on pending entries before allocating a stable id and writing canonical output, but the guard checks target.absPath.includes("/pending/") while store pending paths on Windows use backslashes. A pending path returned by list/seed can therefore enter the layer-flip path, allocate a KP/KT id, write a canonical file, unlink the pending source, and emit layer-change events instead of the normal approve audit path.
fix_direction: Use path-aware detection instead of a POSIX substring, for example normalize target.absPath with toPosixPath/path.relative or infer the area from resolved store roots; add a Windows-shaped regression that modify-layer on knowledge\pending\<type> throws and leaves counters/canonical files unchanged.

title: Store-qualified personal related IDs bypass the KT→KP topology-leak guard

title: fabric install --global ignores the declared --dry-run no-write contract
description: install-v2 declares --dry-run as printing the install plan without writing files, but runInitCommand checks args.global first and immediately calls runGlobalInstall({url}) without passing or honoring dry-run. runGlobalInstall then runs installGlobalCore, writes global ~/.fabric state, and mounts/clones a URL when provided. A user running fabric install --global --dry-run or --global --url ... --dry-run gets real global writes despite the shared dry-run flag contract.
fix_direction: Either reject --dry-run combined with --global as an explicit invalid combination, or extend runGlobalInstall with a planOnly/dryRun mode that reports global config/store clone/mount actions without writing. title: fabric sync accepts --continue and --abort together and silently prioritizes continue
description: sync exposes two mutually exclusive resume actions, --continue and --abort, but the command handler only checks args.continue first and returns before inspecting args.abort. If both flags are supplied, the abort request is ignored and runContinueSync executes. This contradicts the documented conflict-resolution model where continue and abort are alternatives, and can run the more state-advancing path when the user also requested abort.
fix_direction: Add an explicit pre-dispatch guard: if args.continue && args.abort, print an actionable usage error and set a nonzero exit before calling either resume path. Cover the command-level flag combination in tests, not only the lower-level state-machine helpers.

title: Quoted frontmatter arrays do not round-trip through line-based parsers
description: FabReview/FabExtract schemas accept arbitrary strings for tags and relevance_paths, and review also accepts title/summary. Writers now quote complex values with JSON/YAML escaping, but readers still use line-based helpers that split flow arrays on every comma and only strip surrounding quotes. A schema-valid value such as a tag or path containing a comma is serialized as a quoted array element, then parsed back as two corrupted elements; escaped newlines/backslashes in quoted title/summary similarly come back as literal escape text rather than the original string. This breaks list/search filters, path relevance, and metadata round-trip while being distinct from the older unescaped-injection findings.
fix_direction: Replace parseFlowArray/extractInlineArray and stripQuotes/unquote with a real YAML frontmatter parser or a tokenizer that respects quoted scalars and JSON/YAML escapes; add round-trip tests for commas, quotes, backslashes, and escaped newlines. Alternatively constrain schema values to the subset the current parser can actually round-trip.

title: knowledge_deferred ledger events do not identify the deferred entry
description: fab_review.defer mutates each pending file, but the emitted knowledge_deferred event only contains timestamp plus optional until/reason. title: Store executable guard follows store-controlled symlinks outside the store
description: findStoreExecutableViolations walks store contents with statSync(abs), which follows symlinks. A mounted store can include a symlink/junction to a directory outside the store; the guard will recurse into that external tree under an in-store relPath, causing privacy/DoS-prone scanning and false executable violations instead of treating the symlink itself as an untrusted store entry. Existing tests cover script extension, exec bit, .git, depth and entry bounds, but not symlink escape.
fix_direction: Switch to lstatSync for traversal, reject/report symlinks as store violations or resolve realpath and verify it remains under the store root before stat/recurse; add tests for symlink-to-file and symlink-to-directory escaping the store.

title: Write target can resolve outside the project's read-set
description: The resolver contract says a write target is chosen from the read-set, but resolveWriteTarget searches all mountedStores for active/default/route aliases. storeSwitchWrite only validates the alias is mounted and writable, then sets active_write_store/default_write_store without ensuring it is required/bound. A project can therefore write team knowledge into a mounted store that resolveReadSet/cross-store recall does not read, making new entries invisible to the same project until a separate bind happens.
fix_direction: Resolve non-personal write targets from the resolved read-set, or make switch-write/route-write require or automatically add the target to required_stores before saving. Add a regression where active_write_store points to a mounted-but-unbound store and assert writeTarget is null or the store enters readSet.

title: $personal required-store sentinel is accepted but treated as a missing shared store
description: Store/fabric config docs describe suggested_remote '$personal' as a way for a required_store to be satisfied by the implicit personal store, and the schema test accepts that shape. buildStoreResolveInput preserves suggested_remote, but resolveReadSet matches only non-personal mounted stores by req.id and only uses suggested_remote text in the missing_store warning. A valid config like {id:'p', suggested_remote:'$personal'} therefore warns as missing instead of resolving to the mounted personal store.

title: Alias and UUID declarations for the same store duplicate read-set entries
description: required_stores entries are free-form ids and bindRequiredStore dedupes only by exact id. resolveReadSet then loops each entry, matches alias or store_uuid, and pushes a read-set entry every time with no store_uuid dedupe. If a config contains both a store alias and its UUID, the same store is listed/scanned twice; cross-store recall maps readSet.stores directly to directories, so candidates and labels can duplicate and scan work increases.
fix_direction: Normalize required store ids to the matched store_uuid during bind/resolve and skip duplicate store_uuid entries in resolveReadSet while preserving one warning per unresolved id. Add alias+UUID golden coverage asserting one read-set entry.

title: Root fabric --help omits the public uninstall command
description: The root `fabric --help` grouped renderer hardcodes command groups and omits the public `uninstall` command, even though the runtime command registry includes it and the package README lists it as public. title: Hook perf benchmark ignores child process failures
description: `scripts/perf-benchmark.mjs` benchmarkHook destructures only `elapsed` from `measureOnce` and never checks `status`, `signal`, or `stderr`, unlike the CLI benchmark path. A missing or crashing SessionStart hook can still produce fast elapsed samples and pass the p95 gate because fast failures look like good performance.
fix_direction: Make benchmarkHook validate the full `measureOnce` result the same way benchmarkCli does, failing on non-zero status or signal and surfacing stderr. Add a regression fixture with a failing hook to prove the p95 gate fails instead of accepting fast crash samples.

title: Protected-token lint enforces retired project-local knowledge tokens
description: scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts still requires `.fabric/knowledge/` in every packaged SKILL.md and `.fabric/agents.meta.json` in the PROTECTED_TOKENS registry, while project policy has moved knowledge into mounted stores and agents.meta is derived state. Because CI and release both run this lint, removing retired store-only-era wording from skills/bootstrap/protected tokens can fail the gate even after the runtime/docs cleanup is correct. This is distinct from existing issues about stale skill text, the two-step bootstrap token set, and tooling-manifest input paths because the blocker is the gate's hard-coded mandatory token list.
fix_direction: Replace mandatory retired tokens with current store-backed contracts: require fab_recall/current MCP anchors and mounted-store/write-target wording where appropriate, remove `.fabric/knowledge/` as universal SKILL token, remove `.fabric/agents.meta.json` from registry-required tokens unless scoped as legacy/migration-only, and update lint tests plus CI expectations.
